description:
Create a detailed typical road cross-section drawing in LibreCAD of a symmetric two-lane residential street. Save the drawing to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/road_cross_section.dxf. Use feet as units with a 10:1 vertical exaggeration (1 ft vertical = 10 drawing units) and center the road on the vertical axis (X=0).

The drawing must include a 5' wide left sidewalk (X=-21 to -16) with the top surface at Y=5.0, a 2' wide left buffer grass strip, and a 2' wide left curb with the top at Y=5.0. The 12' wide left lane should slope from the center (Y=2.4) to the curb gutter (Y=0). The right side must be a mirror image of the left. Below the road surface, include a pavement structure consisting of 4" Asphalt (3.3 units thick) and 8" Aggregate Base (6.7 units thick), with a subgrade line below the base course.

Organize the drawing using the following layers (Name - Color): PAVEMENT - White, CURB_GUTTER - Yellow, SIDEWALK - Cyan, SUBGRADE - Green, DIMENSIONS - Red, CENTERLINE - Magenta (vertical dashed line at X=0), and ANNOTATIONS - Blue.

Include dimensions for lane widths (12'), total width (24'), and sidewalk width (5'). Add text labels for 'ASPHALT SURFACE', 'AGGREGATE BASE', 'SIDEWALK', 'CURB & GUTTER', and 'CL' at the centerline.
